title: Neomimosa colimensis
taxonomy: Neomimosa colimensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It is a member of the genus Neomimosa, which is part of the legume family. The plant's classification is further broken down into the kingdom Plantae and the phylum Magnoliophyta. Within the phylum, the class Magnoliopsida and the order Fabales also contribute to the plant's classification. Overall, Neomimosa colimensis is a specific and unique plant within the vast and diverse kingdom of Plantae.

intro: Neomimosa colimensis, also known as Mimosa hostilis or simply Mimosa, is a perennial legume native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, providing relief from anxiety, insomnia, and stress due to its adaptogenic and sedative effects. The bioactive constituents of Neomimosa colimensis include 14-acetyl-norharman, a compound with potential neuroprotective and anti-inflammatory properties, as well as other alkaloids and flavonoids. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant are teas and infusions, as well as dried root bark that can be used in decoctions or tinctures for its medicinal benefits.
